original_hypothesis.title: Bot Protection Duration Set But Never Enforced in Transfer Logic
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: AgentToken stores botProtectionDurationInSeconds during initialization but never references it in _transfer, _pretaxValidationAndLimits, or any other transfer-related function. The intended anti-bot protection after initial liquidity addition is completely absent.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The variable is set from supply parameters and stored, but no transfer function checks `block.timestamp < fundedDate + botProtectionDurationInSeconds`. Bots can freely snipe token launches immediately after addInitialLiquidity.

proof_of_signal.pattern: botProtectionDurationInSeconds = supplyParams.botProtectionDurationInSeconds; variable is set but never read in any transfer path

initial_code_evidence.suspicious_location: AgentToken.sol line 99 (set) and lines 385-410 (_pretaxValidationAndLimits - no check)
initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: State variable set but never read in any code path
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Dead code indicates missing security feature
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No bot protection check in transfer logic

analysis_questions: Was maxTokensPerWallet/maxTokensPerTxn also intended but not implemented?
Is bot protection handled in a separate contract not in scope?
What is the economic impact of unprotected launches?
validation_requirements: Search all code for botProtectionDurationInSeconds usage beyond initialization
Confirm no external contract enforces this via _beforeTokenTransfer hook

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Bot monitors for addInitialLiquidity events. Immediately after event, bot buys large amount of tokens at initial price. Regular users buy at inflated prices. Bot sells for profit.
prerequisites: Token launched with bot protection configured

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Sniping bots extract value from every token launch
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Loss is permanent; no mechanism to reverse snipe trades

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test verifying bot protection enforcement during transfer

technical_investigation_focus.capital_requirements: Sufficient to buy tokens at launch
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Large buys right after liquidity add are observable

validation_result.impact_summary: botProtectionDurationInSeconds is stored during initialization but never checked in any transfer path, leaving token launches completely unprotected against sniping bots.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis is confirmed. The variable `botProtectionDurationInSeconds` is declared as a public state variable (AgentToken.sol ~line 25), assigned during `initialize()` (AgentToken.sol ~line 99), but is never referenced in `_transfer`, `_pretaxValidationAndLimits`, `_taxProcessing`, `_autoSwap`, `_eligibleForSwap`, `_beforeTokenTransfer`, or any other function in the contract. The `_pretaxValidationAndLimits` function only checks that transfers to the LP pair don't occur before `fundedDate` is set (i.e., before initial liquidity), but once `fundedDate != 0`, all transfers are allowed without any time-based bot protection. The intended check `block.timestamp < fundedDate + botProtectionDurationInSeconds` is completely absent. Additionally, `maxTokensPerWallet` and `maxTokensPerTxn` from the supply parameters are also never enforced in transfer logic, suggesting a broader pattern of unimplemented protective features.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code evidence confirms the defect. The variable is written once and never read. All transfer-related functions were exhaustively checked. The deployment scripts explicitly configure `BOT_PROTECTION` environment variable, confirming the intent to use this feature.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The variable is set here during initialization but is never read anywhere in the contract. This is the only write to the variable after declaration.

This is the pre-transfer validation function where bot protection should be enforced. There is no check for `block.timestamp < fundedDate + botProtectionDurationInSeconds`. Once fundedDate is set (non-zero), all transfers are permitted without any time-based restriction.
The main transfer function calls _pretaxValidationAndLimits but no bot protection check exists anywhere in the call chain. _beforeTokenTransfer is an empty hook.

calling_sequence: _beforeTokenTransfer(from, to, amount) - empty function, no bot check
_pretaxValidationAndLimits(from, to, amount) - checks fundedDate for LP transfers only, no bot protection duration check
_autoSwap(from, to) - tax swap logic, no bot check
_taxProcessing(applyTax, to, from, amount) - tax calculation, no bot check
Balance updates and Transfer event emitted
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: After addInitialLiquidity sets fundedDate to block.timestamp, any user can immediately buy tokens from the LP pair. The _pretaxValidationAndLimits function at AgentToken.sol ~line 395 only blocks transfers TO the LP pair when fundedDate == 0 (before initial liquidity). Once fundedDate is set, the condition `to_ == uniswapV2Pair && from_ != address(this) && fundedDate == 0` is false, so the check passes. There is no subsequent check anywhere that compares block.timestamp against fundedDate + botProtectionDurationInSeconds.

deployment_assumptions_checked: BOT_PROTECTION environment variable is configured in deployment scripts
config_validation.deployment_evidence: Multiple deployment scripts explicitly set botProtectionDurationInSeconds via BOT_PROTECTION env var: v2/deployFactory.ts calls setTokenSupplyParams with process.env.BOT_PROTECTION, v2/deployVIPFactory.ts does the same, v2/deployMigrator.ts calls setTokenSupplyParams with process.env.BOT_PROTECTION. This confirms operational intent to use bot protection.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack is straightforward and highly realistic: (1) Factory calls executeApplication which creates AgentToken, adds initial liquidity via addInitialLiquidity, setting fundedDate. (2) The addInitialLiquidity transaction (or the executeApplication transaction containing it) is observed in the mempool by a bot. (3) Bot submits a buy transaction immediately after (or in the same block, or even front-runs the LP transfer to lpOwner). (4) Since _pretaxValidationAndLimits only requires fundedDate != 0 for LP-targeted transfers, and no bot protection window is enforced, the bot's purchase succeeds at the initial price. (5) Regular users buy at inflated prices. (6) Bot sells for profit. This is a standard launch sniping attack that the botProtectionDurationInSeconds parameter was clearly designed to prevent.
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM severity. Every AgentToken launch is vulnerable to sniping bots. The bot protection feature was designed, parameterized, configured in deployment scripts, and stored on-chain, but the actual enforcement logic was never implemented. This allows MEV bots to extract value from every token launch by front-running or immediately following the initial liquidity addition. The impact is financial loss for legitimate early buyers who purchase at inflated prices after bot manipulation. The deployment scripts show this is actively configured (BOT_PROTECTION env var), meaning the operators believe protection is in place when it is not.
recommended_mitigations: Add bot protection check in _pretaxValidationAndLimits: if (fundedDate != 0 && block.timestamp < fundedDate + botProtectionDurationInSeconds) { /* apply restrictions such as max buy amount, blacklist non-whitelisted addresses, or revert */ }
Consider also implementing the unused maxTokensPerWallet and maxTokensPerTxn checks that are parameterized in ERC20SupplyParameters but never enforced
Add a comprehensive test that verifies bot protection is active during the configured window after initial liquidity is added
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy AgentToken via factory with botProtectionDurationInSeconds = 3600 (1 hour). 2. Execute application to create token and add initial liquidity. 3. Immediately in the next block, attempt to buy tokens from the Uniswap pair. 4. Verify the buy succeeds, proving no bot protection. 5. Contrast with expected behavior: the buy should have been blocked during the protection window.
uncertainties: It is theoretically possible that an external middleware or off-chain system provides some bot protection outside the smart contract, but no evidence of this exists in the codebase
The intended behavior of the bot protection (block transfers entirely, limit amounts, or some other mechanism) is not documented in code
additional_testing_needed: Verify that maxTokensPerWallet and maxTokensPerTxn are also unenforced (they appear to be stored in supply params but never read)
Check if any proxy or wrapper contract around AgentToken implements bot protection externally
Assess the actual BOT_PROTECTION values used in production to understand the intended protection window
